sanskrit: यथा प्रकाशयत्येकः कृत्स्नं लोकमिमं रविः । क्षेत्रं क्षेत्री तथा कृत्स्नं प्रकाशयति भारत ॥
hindiTranslation: हे भरतवंशोद्भव अर्जुन  जैसे एक ही सूर्य सम्पूर्ण संसारको प्रकाशित करता है, ऐसे ही क्षेत्री क्षेत्रज्ञ, आत्मा सम्पूर्ण क्षेत्रको प्रकाशित करता है ॥
englishTranslation: As one sun illuminates this whole world, so the knower of the field illuminates the whole field, O Bharata.
essence: Awareness illuminates everything without being touched by what it sees.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The one who knows the field makes the whole field known, just as one sun lights up the entire world.
simpleInsight: What reveals your experience is already beyond it.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Krishna is still teaching Arjuna how to see beyond the body and mind. After saying the true self is untouched, he gives a vivid image: the knower of the field illumines the whole field like the sun lights the world.
modernRelevance: You sit alone after a painful argument, replaying every sentence. The mind feels like the whole room — until you notice the quiet awareness in which the replay appears.
translationLiteral: यथा (as) / प्रकाशयति (illuminates) / एकः (one) / कृत्स्नं (whole) / लोकम् (world) / इमम् (this) / रविः (sun) / क्षेत्रम् (field) / क्षेत्री (knower of the field) / तथा (so) / कृत्स्नम् (whole) / प्रकाशयति (illuminates) / भारत (O Bharata)
insight: It shifts attention from the contents of experience to the awareness that makes experience visible.
